title: heracleum sphondylium subsp. cyclocarpum
intro: Heracleum sphondylium subsp. cyclocarpum, also known as wild celery or cow parsley, is a biennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of wild celery include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
